title: PlainCloak v1 message body
description: Normative JSON Schema for the decompressed message body of a PlainCloak v1 wire message. Referenced normatively by spec/v1/06-message-body.md. The schema is the source of truth for field names, types, formats, and the closed object policy. If prose disagrees with this schema, this schema wins. Body field names are single-letter to keep wire size small; their meanings are fixed in spec/v1/06-message-body.md section 6.2.

a.title: Cryptographic suite identifier
a.description: Name of the cryptographic suite used for payload encryption and signature. Members of this enum are mirrored in schemas/v1/algorithms.json. Future v1 updates MUST NOT add suites here without a corresponding entry in the algorithms registry.

i.title: Message identifier
i.description: UUID version 4 in canonical 8-4-4-4-12 textual form, lowercase. See Section 2.5.

t.title: Producer timestamp (Unix milliseconds)
t.description: Producer wall-clock time at message construction. MUST NOT be used by consumers for security decisions. See Section 6.2.4.

s.title: Sender public-key hash
s.description: SHA-256 of the sender's SPKI DER, as 64 lowercase hex characters. See Section 9.

r.title: Recipient public-key hash
r.description: SHA-256 of the recipient's SPKI DER, as 64 lowercase hex characters. See Section 9.

p.title: Encrypted plaintext (Base64)
p.description: Base64-encoded encryption output produced by the suite-specific encryption algorithm. Standard Base64 with padding per RFC 4648 Section 4. The decoded bytes MUST conform to the byte layout fixed by the selected suite (Section 6.2.6): for RSA-OAEP-SHA256 a single ciphertext of the recipient modulus length, for RSA-OAEP-AES256GCM-SHA256 the framed wrapped-key/nonce/ciphertext/tag of Section 8.10.3.

g.title: Signature over the canonical form (Base64)
g.description: Base64-encoded signature produced by the suite-specific signing algorithm over the canonical form (Section 7). Standard Base64 with padding per RFC 4648 Section 4. The decoded length MUST match the suite's expected signature length for the sender's key.
